decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing powerful control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while also maintaining its own board presence through efficient creatures and removal spells. It aims to slow down the game and grind out incremental advantages until it can establish a dominant position with cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The mana base is designed to provide consistent access to both black and green mana sources, allowing for a versatile range of spells to be cast. The deck excels in longer, grindy matchups where it can outvalue and outlast the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang Parhelion as a central combo piece to generate advantage and control the board. The deck includes cards that support the combo strategy, such as Formidable Speaker and Raffine's Informant, while also including removal and disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command. The deck aims to ramp up mana quickly with cards like Multiversal Passage and Cache Grab to cast powerful threats like Parhelion II and Skysovereign, Consul Flagship. The overall goal is to establish board dominance and overwhelm the opponent with powerful synergies.

The main focus of this deck is to assemble and protect the combo pieces, namely Greasefang and Parhelion II, in order to establish a board state that is difficult for the opponent to interact with. Cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help control the opponent's hand and board, while Monument to Endurance can provide additional protection. The deck also includes cards like Lively Dirge and Pest Control to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The mana base includes a mix of lands to ensure consistency and to support the various color requirements of the deck.

This deck focuses on a control strategy that aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while building up board presence and resources. It utilizes cards like Duress and Thoughtseize to interact with the opponent's hand, while also having removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to deal with threats on the board. The deck also includes mana ramp in the form of Llanowar Elves and Mutavault to help play out its powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The key is to outvalue and outlast the opponent through a combination of disruption and strong creature threats.

The deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang Parhelion's abilities to create various powerful combos that can overwhelm the opponent quickly. It includes a mix of creatures, spells, and lands that support the strategy of generating tokens, gaining life, and disrupting the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent through a combination of aggressive plays and control elements, ultimately leading to a victory through its synergy and versatility.

This deck aims to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Arboreal Grazer and Lotus Field to cast powerful spells like Emergent Ultimatum and Omniscience. The key strategy involves untapping Lotus Field multiple times using Hidden Strings and Vizier of Tumbling Sands to generate an overwhelming amount of mana and cast game-winning spells. The deck also includes toolbox cards like Fae of Wishes to fetch answers from the sideboard and combo pieces like Thespian's Stage to create infinite mana loops. Overall, the deck focuses on assembling its combo pieces efficiently to overwhelm the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize a combination of aggressive creatures and efficient mana production to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By playing powerful creatures like Resplendent Angel and Righteous Valkyrie, along with cards that provide additional benefits like Bishop of Wings and Giada, Font of Hope, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and maintain it throughout the game. Cards like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition provide additional flexibility and removal options, making it a well-rounded and strong aggro deck in the Selesnya color combination.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board through a combination of removal spells and hand disruption while also utilizing creatures with powerful abilities to apply pressure on the opponent. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and slow down their game plan, ultimately out-valuing them through efficient removal and strong creature presence. With a mix of hand disruption, removal spells, and resilient threats, this deck aims to grind out incremental advantages over the course of the game to emerge victorious.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the Greasefang Parhelion combo with cards like Fleeting Spirit and Godless Shrine. The deck also includes various removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board. The inclusion of Thoughtseize helps disrupt the opponent's game plan, while cards like Iron-Shield Elf and Monument to Endurance provide protection and resilience. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and efficiently to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo by utilizing cards like Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Formidable Speaker to search for key pieces. The deck also includes disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Once the combo is assembled, the deck can generate a powerful board presence with Parhelion II and other support cards like Esika's Chariot and Iron-Shield Elf. Thundering Broodwagon and Voice of Victory provide additional ways to win the game once the combo is set up.

The deck focuses on utilizing cheap, aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear, Soul-Scar Mage, and Cori-Steel Cutter to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also includes burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to control the board and remove threats. The deck aims to maintain tempo and pressure the opponent with a fast-paced playstyle, utilizing cards like Stormchaser's Talent and Boomerang Basics to close out games quickly. The inclusion of Riverpyre Verge and Den of the Bugbear provides additional utility and flexibility in the mana base.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a mix of control elements to disrupt opponents. It aims to quickly build up a board presence using efficient creatures like Drannith Magistrate and Aven Interrupter, while also utilizing disruptive spells like Spell Queller and Spell Snare to control the opponent's plays. Consult the Star Charts and March of Otherworldly Light provide card advantage and utility, while cards like No More Lies and Soul Partition offer protection and resilience. The deck's diverse land base ensures consistent mana access for its aggressive and disruptive strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive and fast-paced plays. The deck focuses on dealing direct damage to the opponent's creatures and life points, utilizing spells like Burst Lightning, Lightning Strike, and Reckless Rage. Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage help to boost the deck's prowess in dealing damage quickly. The inclusion of Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins adds additional damage sources, while Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Screaming Nemesis provide ongoing threats. Overall, the deck aims to establish early board presence and continuously pressure the opponent to secure a quick victory.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using a variety of disruptive spells, removal, and counterspells to control the game and outvalue the opponent. With a combination of versatile threats and cards that can generate card advantage, the deck aims to grind out the opponent and establish dominance over the game. Yorion, Sky Nomad provides additional value with blink effects, while powerful finishers like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and The Meathook Massacre help secure the victory once the opponent is locked out of the game.

This deck focuses on using low-cost spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. By utilizing prowess triggers and efficient removal, it aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while controlling the board. The inclusion of card draw and mana fixing ensures consistency in both aggression and resource management. With a streamlined mana base and focused game plan, this deck is built to be fast and versatile in the Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

This deck relies on a diverse range of powerful cards to control the game and outvalue opponents. With a focus on utilizing Niv-Mizzet Reborn's ability to draw powerful multicolored cards, the deck is able to adapt to different situations and answer threats efficiently. The inclusion of various removal spells, ramp cards, and versatile threats allow the deck to maintain control of the game and eventually overpower the opponent. The deck's consistency and versatility make it a challenging and rewarding archetype to pilot in Pioneer 1 vs 1.
